title: Signal Tanker Weekly Report
summary:
Main
Data Source: The Signal Ocean Platform, Toncharts - Demand https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/tanker-reports-toncharts/2nq6c1/306623343?h=peUdQrUawvxIYaF--sijeEvPracsW6atxQ4ADWeuSJQ https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/tanker-reports-vesselSpeeds/2nq6c4/306623343?h=peUdQrUawvxIYaF--sijeEvPracsW6atxQ4ADWeuSJQ
The second week of March confirmed stronger momentum in VLCC rates, with demand growth paving the way for further optimism as supply also follows a declining trend. Overall, the first two months of the year saw a surprising increase in VLCC demand (see image above) in tonne-days, consistent with stronger economic signs in China and expectations for an upward trend in oil imports from the world's second-largest country this year. In response to the recent upturn, the ballast speed of VLCC tankers surpassed the levels of the previous two years and is now above 12 knots, with a similar increase on the horizon for the first quarter in the coming days.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-03-10T175700.958.png | assets/2023-03-11_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-03-10t175700958_9aa03037365f.png
Data Source: The Signal Ocean Platform, Toncharts - Demand https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/tanker-reports-toncharts/2nq6c1/306623343?h=peUdQrUawvxIYaF--sijeEvPracsW6atxQ4ADWeuSJQ https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/tanker-reports-vesselSpeeds/2nq6c4/306623343?h=peUdQrUawvxIYaF--sijeEvPracsW6atxQ4ADWeuSJQ
The second week of March confirmed stronger momentum in VLCC rates, with demand growth paving the way for further optimism as supply also follows a declining trend. Overall, the first two months of the year saw a surprising increase in VLCC demand (see image above) in tonne-days, consistent with stronger economic signs in China and expectations for an upward trend in oil imports from the world's second-largest country this year.

children.title: ‘ Dirty ' VLCC , Suezmax, Aframax- Firmer
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-03-10T175742.984.png | assets/2023-03-11_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-03-10t175742984_6c5e97a2c082.png
Crude oil freight rate sentiment improved in the second week of March with a steady increase in the VLCC and Aframax segments. VLCC AG-FE freight rates were at WS72, almost 27 points higher than at the end of the second week.

children.title: ‘ Product ' LR2 - Firmer | LR1 - Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-03-10T175942.308.png | assets/2023-03-11_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-03-10t175942308_b4eb7735a591.png
LR2 AG freight rates rose to WS195, 20 points higher than two weeks ago. LR1 MEG -to-Japan freight rates posted a weaker trend, falling below WS190 in the first two weeks of March.

children.title: ‘ Clean ' MR2, MR1 - Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-03-10T180017.084.png | assets/2023-03-11_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-03-10t180017084_544bc73811ea.png
MR1 rates Algeria-to-Med are now at WS180, 30 points lower than at the end of February. MR1 rates Baltic-to-Cont are now at WS173 and have dropped to levels similar to mid-February.

children.title: ‘ Dirty ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
section_path: ‘ Dirty ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
children.section_path_text: ‘ Dirty ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
children.summary:
VLCC - Decreasing | Suezmax - Aframax - Increasing
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-03-10T180139.706.png | assets/2023-03-11_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-03-10t180139706_b1553aa10738.png
The supply of crude oil tankers has increased significantly in the Suezmax and Aframax segments, while the number of vessels in the VLCC segment is still below the annual average. VLCC Ras Tanura : The number of vessels is now around 60, 17 less than the annual average.

children.title: ‘ Clean ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas | LR2 | Increasing
section_path: ‘ Clean ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas | LR2 | Increasing
children.section_path_text: ‘ Clean ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas | LR2 | Increasing

children.title: ‘ Clean ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas | MR1 | Decreasing
section_path: ‘ Clean ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas | MR1 | Decreasing
children.section_path_text: ‘ Clean ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas | MR1 | Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-03-10T180259.630.png | assets/2023-03-11_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-03-10t180259630_2463263f27ae.png
Clean LR2 AG Jubail: The number of vessels held at a level around 11, with an increasing trend in the next days of March, but still not above the annual average of 13. Clean MR1 Algeria Skikda: The number of vessels decreased to 39, 12 less than the previous week and 4 above the annual average.

children.title: ‘ Dirty ' | VLCC - Suezmax - Increasing | Aframax - Decreasing
section_path: ‘ Dirty ' | VLCC - Suezmax - Increasing | Aframax - Decreasing
children.section_path_text: ‘ Dirty ' | VLCC - Suezmax - Increasing | Aframax - Decreasing

children.title: ‘ Clean ' | Panamax - Decreasing | MR2 - MR1 - Decreasing
section_path: ‘ Clean ' | Panamax - Decreasing | MR2 - MR1 - Decreasing
children.section_path_text: ‘ Clean ' | Panamax - Decreasing | MR2 - MR1 - Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-03-10T180407.000.png | assets/2023-03-11_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-03-10t180407000_d61e199b025d.png
Dirty demand ton-days: March began with slowing momentum in the crude oil tanker segment for Aframax vessels but with significant strength in the VLCC and Suezmax segments. Clean demand ton-days / Panamax demand: After several consecutive increases in the second half of February, there was a sharp decline with a tendency for a further decline in the coming days.
